events.title: Hormuz conflict impacts shipping fuel strategy
events.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, leading to increased operational costs and a shift in the viability of alternative fuels in the shipping industry.

events.title: Indonesian coal exports decline in 2026
events.description: Seaborne coal exports from Indonesia have declined in the first four months of 2026, according to Banchero Costa. The drop in exports is based on vessel tracking data and may indicate a shift in global coal demand or supply dynamics.

events.title: Ukrainian drone explodes in Romanian port
events.description: A Ukrainian sea drone exploded in Constanta Harbor, Romania. No injuries were reported, but the incident is under investigation.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces draft limits due to El Niño
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority announced a reduction in the maximum authorized draft for Neopanamax vessels due to the El Niño weather forecast, which could impact transit efficiency and cargo capacity.

events.title: Ukrainian attacks on cargo ships kill Azerbaijani seafarers
events.description: Five Azerbaijani seafarers were killed and several injured in Ukrainian attacks on commercial ships in the Black Sea region, disrupting freight flows and raising safety concerns for maritime operations.

events.title: U.S. reports higher Hormuz Strait vessel transits
events.description: U.S. Central Command reports nearly 1,000 commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz in two months, exceeding private tracking data that relies on ship transponders.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shutdown raises oil market uncertainty
events.description: Oil market experts are divided on the impact of a potential prolonged shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit chokepoint, as U.S. oil inventories fall to a 22-year low.

events.title: US boards sanctioned stateless tanker with Iranian oil
events.description: The United States has boarded a sanctioned stateless tanker in the Indian Ocean, reportedly carrying Iranian oil. The action was taken by U.S. Indo-Pacific Command as part of ongoing efforts to interdict vessels linked to Iran.

events.title: Omani Oil Exports Resume After Drone Attack
events.description: Oman's oil export terminal at Mina Al Fahal resumed normal operations on June 5 following a drone attack that had temporarily disrupted exports.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd discontinues CCM service to Mexico/Central America
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd announced it will discontinue its CCM service connecting Mexico and Central America, with the final sailing scheduled for July 2026. The service currently calls at Lázaro Cárdenas, Acajutla, and Corinto.

events.title: Panama Canal Neopanamax draft limit reduction
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority will reduce the maximum draft for Neopanamax vessels starting July 3 due to concerns over potential El Niño conditions, which could impact transit capacity and shipping efficiency.

events.title: Mercuria uses government deals to move oil through Hormuz
events.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. has successfully moved oil through the Strait of Hormuz via government-brokered deals, according to legal filings, highlighting how traders are managing the critical chokepoint amid the Iran war.

events.title: U.S. Boards Iranian Sanctioned Tanker in Indian Ocean
events.description: U.S. forces boarded a sanctioned Iranian tanker in the Indian Ocean as part of an expanded maritime enforcement campaign targeting Iran's oil trade. The action reflects heightened tensions and potential disruptions to regional shipping routes.

events.title: Hormuz Crisis Disrupts Container Shipping
events.description: Container freight rates are surging due to the conflict in the Middle East and disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. Rising tensions and energy crisis fears are compounding the situation during peak shipping season.

events.title: Iran charges toll for Hormuz Strait transit
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is charging $2 million per voyage for transit authorization through the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting standard shipping procedures and raising concerns over increased costs and potential geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Hormuz transit payments impact diplomatic tensions
events.description: Below-threshold transit payments are affecting diplomatic relations and freight flows through the strategic Strait of Hormuz before any formal agreement is reached.

events.title: India-Gulf container rates drop due to eased backlogs
events.description: Container shipping rates from India to the Persian Gulf have dropped by up to 40% as capacity returns and cargo backlogs ease. Current rates are around $2,100 per 20ft and $3,200 per 40ft container.

events.title: Container spot rates surge due to peak season and tight capacity
events.description: Spot rates for container shipping on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes have risen sharply amid peak season demand and constrained capacity. Carriers have implemented higher FAK rates and new peak season surcharges for contracted shippers.

events.title: US forces board sanctioned tanker Davina in Indian Ocean
events.description: U.S. forces conducted an interdiction of the sanctioned stateless oil tanker Davina in the Indian Ocean, according to the U.S. military's Indo-Pacific Command.

events.title: Singapore secures LNG supply to offset Qatar disruption
events.description: Singapore has secured replacement LNG cargoes to offset disrupted supplies from Qatar through 2026, according to the Energy Market Authority. Qatar previously supplied about 10% of Singapore's LNG needs before the Middle East conflict.

events.title: Fuel availability tight in Houston
events.description: Heavy congestion and delays at Houston fuel loading terminals are causing tight availability for bunker fuel, with lead times of 8-10 days for HSFO and VLSFO.

events.title: Containership transits through Suez Canal slow
events.description: Containership transits through the Suez Canal have slowed, with 20 vessels passing in the week ended 24 May and 26 in the following week, according to the Drewry Red Sea Diversion Tracker. This indicates a continued but reduced impact of the Red Sea diversion trend on global freight flows.

events.title: MSC reinstates Transpacific Pearl service
events.description: MSC is reintroducing its Transpacific Pearl service on the Asia–US trade lane due to improved market demand, following its withdrawal in mid-2025.

events.title: Sanctioned Russian LNG carrier transits Arctic route
events.description: The sanctioned Russian LNG carrier Christophe de Margerie made a rare early-season eastbound transit along the Northern Sea Route, escorted by the nuclear icebreaker Ural. This marks only the second time such a voyage has occurred at this time of year, highlighting potential shifts in Arctic shipping activity.

events.title: Middle East conflict impacts bunker fuel market
events.description: The global bunker market remains volatile due to ongoing uncertainty from the Middle East conflict, affecting fuel prices and trade stability.

events.title: Container spot rates surge due to Red Sea disruptions and peak season
events.description: Container shipping spot rates have surged to their highest in years due to Red Sea disruptions, early peak season demand, and aggressive carrier pricing. The Shanghai Containerized Freight Index (SCFI) rose 155 points to 2,726.48.

events.title: European mine-clearance force forms for Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A European mine-clearance force is being assembled for the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States. The move signals potential risks to maritime traffic in this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: GEODIS opens new port hub in Le Havre
events.description: GEODIS has launched its first integrated port hub in Le Havre, France, consolidating its four business lines into a single logistics platform at the country's largest container port to enhance import and export operations.

events.title: Panama Canal congestion at yearly high
events.description: Panama Canal congestion has reached its highest level this year, leading to Jones Act waivers for domestic shipments. The Panama Canal Authority plans to maintain full transit capacity through December despite maintenance and El Niño risks.

events.title: Hormuz Strait traffic in potential limbo post-conflict
events.description: Experts suggest that even after the conflict in Iran ends, control of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz may remain in a gray zone, neither fully closed nor fully open, potentially disrupting long-term freight flows.

events.title: Cargo ship seized in Sweden over Ukraine grain theft
events.description: A Swedish court approved the seizure of a cargo ship accused of stealing grain from occupied regions of Ukraine. The vessel is being held pending legal action related to alleged theft and smuggling activities.

events.title: Ukraine Sea Corridor Exports Top 200 Million Tons
events.description: Ukraine reports that over 200 million tons of exports have been shipped via the Black Sea corridor since its establishment in September 2023, indicating improved freight flow despite ongoing conflict.

events.title: Tariff fraud enforcement targets importers
events.description: Federal authorities are taking enforcement actions against importers, including First Brands, Perfectus, Canadian steel firms, and Greenbrier, for alleged duty evasion. This reflects increased scrutiny and potential disruptions in import compliance and trade flows.

events.title: Kuwait oil production recovery after Hormuz reopening
events.description: Kuwait Petroleum Company expects to restore 70% of its oil production within 6-8 weeks following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy chokepoint.

events.title: MSC vessel hit by projectiles at Um Qasr port
events.description: MSC's vessel MSC Sariska V was struck by two projectiles at Iraq's Um Qasr port amid rising Middle East tensions. The incident occurred during a stop at the port on Monday.

events.title: France releases detained tanker captain
events.description: French prosecutors confirmed the release of the captain of the tanker Tagor, which was detained by France on May 31 for suspected shadow fleet operations.

events.title: FedEx partner airline seeks FAA waiver for Caribbean routes
events.description: A FedEx air cargo contractor is at risk of losing Caribbean service due to FAA regulations limiting how far larger aircraft can fly over water. The airline is seeking a waiver to continue operations with bigger planes on these routes.

events.title: US imposes forced labour tariffs on EU and 59 countries
events.description: The US has rejected EU objections to new 10%-12.5% tariffs on imports from the EU and 59 other countries, citing failures to address forced labour in supply chains. This policy move could disrupt trade flows and increase costs for affected imports.

events.title: Mine-hunting drone deployed in Hormuz coalition
events.description: The Royal Navy has deployed a new mine-hunting drone in the Strait of Hormuz as part of a 15-nation coalition to clear naval mines and ensure safe passage for shipping.

events.title: Oil shipments increase through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has increased as traders use stealth measures to navigate the area. This reflects ongoing uncertainty in the energy market amid the Iran war.

events.title: Persian Gulf attacks disrupt oil flows
events.description: Fresh attacks in the Persian Gulf have raised concerns over the resumption of energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint. The situation has pushed oil prices higher and created uncertainty in energy markets.

events.title: UAE becomes India's top crude supplier after Saudi
events.description: India's imports from UAE surpassed Saudi Arabia in May as oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz remained disrupted for a third consecutive month. This shift reflects rerouting of crude supplies amid ongoing disruptions.

events.title: COSCO launches direct Poland-Asia shipping service
events.description: COSCO SHIPPING Lines is launching the AEU7 service, establishing the first direct container route between Poland and Asian markets such as Vietnam, Malaysia, and Hong Kong. The service will begin with calls at the Baltic Hub in Gdańsk by the end of August 2026.

events.title: CMA CGM imposes peak season surcharges on Asia-North Europe routes
events.location: Asia to North Europe shipping routes
events.description: CMA CGM has announced a US$600 per TEU Peak Season Surcharge effective June 15, 2026, for cargo from Asian ports to North Europe, impacting trade corridors during a critical shipping period.

events.title: Major U.S. shipping surcharge increase
events.description: A major liner has imposed a $2,600 surcharge on a U.S. shipping service amid heightened demand during the peak shipping season, signaling significant industry adjustments.

events.title: Transpacific and Asia-Europe freight rate decline
events.description: Spot freight rates on the transpacific and Asia-Europe trades have declined after an initial spike following the Hormuz closure, due to weak demand and healthy supply. Stakeholders are reacting strongly to carrier behavior and rate volatility.

events.title: Intra-Asia shipping rates surge due to Middle East bottlenecks
events.description: Shipping bottlenecks in the Middle East are causing delays and rerouting, leading to a significant increase in intra-Asia container rates, with some routes seeing over 40% rate hikes.

events.title: European Cargo enters administration due to financial pressures
events.description: European Cargo, a Bournemouth-based freighter airline, entered administration on 3 June due to rising operating costs, working capital pressures, and reduced flying activity. The collapse highlights financial instability in the freight sector amid challenging market conditions.

events.title: Maersk fined $1.9M for unfair D&D fees
events.description: The US Federal Maritime Commission fined Maersk $1.9 million for unfair detention and demurrage (D&D) fees. Additional carriers, including Cosco and Wan Hai Lines, are under scrutiny for similar practices.

events.title: Mine-clearing mission launched in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The UK and France have finalized plans to lead a multinational mine-clearing mission in the Strait of Hormuz following an agreement between the US and Iran to reopen the waterway. The mission aims to restore safe passage for global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: MSC vessel struck by projectiles in Iraq
events.description: MSC confirmed its vessel MSC Sariska V was struck by two projectiles while departing Um-Qasr Port in Iraq, raising concerns about regional maritime security and potential disruptions to shipping.

events.title: Port of Koper adds new direct Asia service via MSC
events.description: The Port of Koper has launched a new direct Asia service operated by MSC, enhancing its connectivity with the Mediterranean and Asia. The bi-weekly PHOENIX service began with the arrival of the MSC AGAMEMNON VIII on 31 May.

events.title: CMA CGM raises PSS on key trade routes
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) across trade lanes to Africa, Europe, and North America, including routes from China to South Africa, effective 7 June 2026, reflecting ongoing carrier pricing adjustments ahead of peak season.

events.title: Freight train fatalities in US
events.description: Five people were killed and two injured in four separate freight train collisions across the US, including incidents near San Antonio and Ypsilanti, Michigan, over the past week.

events.title: EU proposes mine-clearing mission in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The European Union has proposed that its Aspides naval mission take the lead in clearing mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, as part of a Franco-British initiative.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Tensions and Iranian Dark Fleet Enforcement
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is experiencing heightened tensions as Iran's dark fleet activity stalls and U.S. enforcement actions expand, targeting Iranian maritime logistics and shadow fleet operations.

events.title: Atlantic MR freight rate divergence
events.description: In late May, MR product tanker freight rates in the Atlantic showed divergence, with UKC-USAC routes weakening while USG-UKC benchmarks surged amid broader tanker market pressures.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz tensions disrupt shipping
events.description: Geopolitical tensions, including Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz since its war with Israel and the US began on Feb. 28, have disrupted maritime traffic and pushed up freight rates across multiple sectors.

events.title: Iran Accuses US of Attack on MSC Boxship
events.description: Iranian officials accused the United States of violating a ceasefire and attacking an oil tanker, with the IRGC reportedly targeting an MSC boxship and a US Navy headquarters in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: France detains Russian tanker captain
events.description: French prosecutors have taken into custody the captain of a shadow fleet tanker detained on Sunday. The incident is part of ongoing scrutiny of vessels linked to sanctioned entities.

events.title: LNG Strike Disrupts Exports in Australia
events.description: A strike at Australian LNG operations has delayed loading and exports, impacting the departure of the LNG carrier Pacific Breeze after a work slowdown.

events.title: Ukrainian strike damages Russian frigate in Kronstadt
events.description: Ukraine's strike on St. Petersburg damaged a Russian frigate in drydock at Kronstadt's main harbor, demonstrating extended drone capabilities and escalating military tensions.

events.title: U.S. imposes new tariffs on 60 countries over forced labor
events.description: The U.S. has imposed new tariffs of up to 12.5% on imports from 60 countries it claims have failed to address forced labor in their supply chains. The move could disrupt global trade flows and impact freight and logistics operations.

events.title: US military supports Hormuz Strait shipping quietly
events.description: The US military is using discreet methods to protect commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, following the abandoned Project Freedom. The move aims to ensure safe passage through this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: EU Considers Aspides Mission for Hormuz Mine Clearing
events.description: The European Union is considering expanding its Aspides naval mission to take a leading role in mine-clearing operations in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, as part of a Franco-British initiative.

events.title: EU considers freezing Russian oil price cap due to Middle East tensions
events.description: The European Union is considering freezing its price cap on Russian oil amid rising tensions in the Middle East and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which are driving crude prices higher and increasing concerns over global energy supply stability.

events.title: India's fuel exports impacted by Ukraine and Iran conflicts
events.description: India's fuel exports hit a record monthly low in May due to disruptions from the conflicts in Ukraine and West Asia. However, there are signs of a potential rebound as some Western nations reconsider their fuel sourcing amid ongoing supply shortages.

events.title: EU LNG imports decline due to Middle East war disruptions
events.description: LNG imports into the European Union fell 8% year-on-year in May as ongoing disruptions from the Middle East war continue to impact global energy trade.

events.title: Bunker Fuel Supply Tightens in Singapore and Malaysia
events.description: Bunker fuel availability for Very Low Sulphur Fuel Oil (VLSFO) and High Sulphur Fuel Oil (HSFO) in Singapore and Malaysia is under pressure, with extended lead times reported. Suppliers now recommend 10-15 days for VLSFO and HSFO, up from previous ranges, potentially affecting vessel operations in the region.

events.title: Yemeni Government Tightens Control Over Red Sea Coast
events.description: The Yemeni government is increasing its control over the Red Sea coastline, which has become a critical alternative route for shipping due to the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz. This move could impact freight flows and port operations in the region.

events.title: U.S. Senate urged to act on cargo theft bill
events.description: A coalition of retail, business, and supply-chain groups is urging the U.S. Senate to act on a cargo theft bill passed by the House. The legislation aims to address ongoing issues with cargo theft impacting freight flows.

events.title: India reconsiders relaxed cabotage rules for foreign ships
events.description: India is re-evaluating its relaxed cabotage rules that allow foreign-flagged vessels to carry domestic and international cargo on coastal routes. The move comes amid pushback from industry stakeholders and could impact domestic shipping competition and logistics flows.

events.title: MSC acquires controlling stake in Ukraine's Yuzhny terminal
events.description: MSC has acquired a 51% stake in Ukraine's Transinvestservice Container Terminal at Yuzhny, expanding its European port network. The deal is reported to be finalized but subject to confirmation.

events.title: US to impose new tariffs on EU, UK, and others over forced labor
events.description: The US plans to impose 10% to 12.5% tariffs on goods from the EU, UK, and others for failing to address forced labor in supply chains, potentially disrupting trade flows.

events.title: Rising aircargo rates hurt Bangladesh perishable exports
events.description: Surging airfreight rates are severely impacting Bangladesh's perishable exports, particularly to Europe and the Middle East, reducing their global competitiveness amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Tankers gather near Hormuz Strait
events.description: Greek oil tanker owners are positioning vessels near the Persian Gulf, anticipating potential high earnings if the Strait of Hormuz reopens. The movement reflects uncertainty and potential disruption at this key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: TotalEnergies exits Russian Arctic LNG 2 project
events.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has approved TotalEnergies' exit from the Arctic LNG 2 project, signaling continued Western disengagement from Russian energy assets amid sanctions and geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike St. Petersburg Oil Terminal
events.description: Ukrainian drones attacked the St. Petersburg Oil Terminal in Russia, breaching air defenses and causing damage. The incident could disrupt oil logistics and trade flows in the region.

events.title: Marinakis advocates paying fees for Hormuz transits
events.description: Prominent Greek tanker owner John Marinakis suggests it is better to pay a fee for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting ongoing concerns about transits through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure spiking container rates
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed nearly 100 days after the start of the Iran war, causing upward pressure on container freight rates as carriers pass costs to shippers. Periodic reports of a potential agreement to reopen the waterway have not materialized, and ongoing conflict continues to disrupt freight flows.

events.title: Behram Box container ship delivered after 2008 crisis
events.description: The 917 teu Behram Box container ship, whose construction was abandoned during the 2008 financial crisis, has been delivered and chartered to Unifeeder, marking a revival of a long-stalled Turkish shipbuilding project.

events.title: MSC acquires stake in Pivdennyi container terminal
events.description: Mediterranean Shipping Co (MSC) has reportedly acquired a 51% stake in the Transinvestservice Container Terminal in Pivdennyi, Ukraine. This marks MSC's second container terminal investment in the region.

events.title: US fires missile at Iranian crude tanker near Kharg Island
events.description: The US Navy fired a Hellfire missile into the engine room of a crude tanker heading toward Iran's Kharg Island, leading to retaliatory strikes by Iran. The incident risks escalating tensions and disrupting oil shipments in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption impacts shipping
events.description: Shipping industry figures, including John Marinakis, have commented on the impact of the recent closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to regional conflict, suggesting a transit fee might have been preferable to prolonged disruption.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Disrupts Energy Supply Chains
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed to commercial traffic, causing severe strain on energy supply chains and testing the global maritime industry's ability to maintain freight flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Sparks Supply Concerns
events.description: Ongoing exchanges of fire between U.S. and Iranian forces near the Strait of Hormuz have prompted warnings from oil producers about declining reserves due to disrupted shipments.

events.title: U.S. Disables Iran-Linked Tanker Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. forces have disabled an Iran-linked tanker near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, raising concerns over regional stability and potential disruptions to oil shipments.

events.title: Texas allows CDLs for temporary farm workers
events.description: Texas has resumed issuing non-domiciled commercial drivers licenses to legally authorized foreign workers, impacting labor availability in the freight sector.

events.title: U.S. Disables Sanctioned Tanker Bound for Iran
events.description: U.S. forces disabled a sanctioned oil tanker attempting to reach an Iranian port, the sixth such vessel interdicted under Washington's maritime blockade. The action disrupts freight flows and escalates tensions in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz internet cable fee threat
events.description: Iran has raised concerns about imposing fees on undersea internet cables passing through the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting a potential risk to 20% of global data flows and prompting attention from tech firms and investors.

events.title: Asia-US container rates surge amid Middle East ceasefire talks
events.location: East Asia to US West Coast
events.description: Container shipping rates from East Asia and China to the US have risen sharply this week, with some analysts reporting double-digit increases. The rise comes as negotiations continue to extend the ceasefire in the Middle East, which is affecting tanker rates but not container flows.

events.title: Panama Canal 1A Booking Open to Neopanamax Vessels
events.description: Starting July 1, 2026, HML D Neopanamax vessels can request 1A booking slots for transit through the Panama Canal. This update modifies the transit reservation system.

events.title: Chinese coal mine explosion disrupts supply
events.description: A deadly gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China revealed illegal labor practices and unreported coal sales, raising concerns about tighter oversight and potential extended supply disruptions.

events.title: U.S. reduces steel and aluminum import tariffs
events.description: U.S. President Donald Trump signed a proclamation reducing Section 232 tariffs on certain steel and aluminum derivative products from 25% to 15%. The change affects imports of items like agricultural machinery and residential heating equipment.

events.title: Swire Shipping restricts 40ft container acceptance at PNG ports
events.description: Swire Shipping has immediately stopped accepting 40-foot container shipments to and from Vanimo and Lorengau in Papua New Guinea, while 20-foot containers remain unaffected. The move may impact cargo planning for customers using these ports.

events.title: EU prepares new trade instrument against Chinese goods
events.description: The European Union is developing a new trade instrument to limit the influx of Chinese industrial goods, which could lead to retaliatory actions from Beijing and impact global trade flows.

events.title: Ocean container rates surge $1,000 in one week
events.description: Benchmark ocean container rates increased by $1,000 in a single week due to rate hikes and surcharges, driven by the peak shipping season. This sharp rise indicates significant strain on global freight capacity and pricing.

events.title: Border Patrol arrests 36 truck drivers in Arizona
events.description: U.S. Border Patrol arrested 36 truck drivers during a five-day operation in Arizona, focusing on their immigration and licensing status, signaling increased federal enforcement impacting freight operations.

events.title: North Sea strike on Ithaca Energy assets
events.description: Workers on Ithaca Energy's North Sea assets, employed by Bilfinger, are set to strike in June over pay disputes. The industrial action involves the Alba FSU and FPF1 facilities and could disrupt offshore energy operations.

events.title: U.S. Stands Firm on No Sanctions Relief for Hormuz Strait Reopening
events.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated that no Iran sanctions relief has been offered in exchange for reopening the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing that any relief is conditional on Iran abandoning its nuclear program. This policy stance could prolong uncertainty in one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints.

events.title: CMA CGM Port Congestion Surcharge for Freetown
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced a Port Congestion Surcharge of $600 per TEU for cargo destined for Freetown, Sierra Leone, effective from June 2026, due to ongoing port congestion issues.

events.title: MSC imposes Peak Season Surcharges on transatlantic trade lanes
events.description: MSC has announced Peak Season Surcharges on cargo moving from West Mediterranean and Adriatic ports to North America, effective July 1, 2026. The surcharge applies to all container types, signaling potential cost increases during peak shipping periods.

events.title: ONE enhances Asia-South Africa shipping services
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) has announced service enhancements to its Asia-South Africa routes, including weekly frequency and updated port rotations, aiming to improve reliability and efficiency on this trade corridor.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic remains disrupted despite peace deal
events.description: Shipping executives at Posidonia 2026 indicate that diplomatic agreements between Washington and Tehran are not yet restoring commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting ongoing concerns about safety and stability in the region.

events.title: ANL adjusts rotation due to New Zealand port delays
events.description: ANL has modified the rotation of Xin Zhang Zhou voyages 076N and 077S due to vessel delays at New Zealand ports, adding a call to Hong Kong after Ningbo to prevent schedule disruptions.

events.title: Fog causes port congestion in Shanghai and Ningbo
events.description: Dense fog has worsened congestion at Shanghai and Ningbo ports, increasing ship waiting times and delaying cargo bound for Europe and the US. Linerlytica estimates 1.5 million teu of shipping capacity is currently held up in North Asian ports.

events.title: Iran attack on MSC box ship near Umm Qasr
events.description: MSC confirmed its vessel MSC Sariska V was struck by two projectiles near Umm Qasr, Iraq. The attack occurred as the ship departed port, with one projectile hitting the vessel during pilot boarding and another impacting the crew area shortly after.

events.title: US proposes 25% tariffs on Brazilian shippers
events.description: US regulators proposed a 25% tariff on Brazilian shippers, citing issues including anti-corruption enforcement, intellectual property, ethanol market access, and illegal deforestation, adding uncertainty for freight operations.

events.title: Middle East conflict disrupts airfreight
events.description: Air cargo rates increased by 36% year on year in May due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East, disrupting global supply chains and causing significant market shifts since early March.

events.title: LNG Tanker Disappears Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: An LNG tanker, the Al Rayyan, turned off its transponder before crossing the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about disruptions to global gas trade amid heightened tensions.

events.title: Fayard Shipyard Supports Russia's Yamal LNG Fleet Amid Sanctions
events.description: Denmark’s Fayard Shipyard continues to provide critical maintenance for Russia’s Yamal LNG fleet as European sanctions pressure other Western shipyards to disengage.

events.title: Containership Hit by Projectiles Off Iraq
events.description: MSC's containership MSC Sariska V was struck by two projectiles while departing Iraq's Port of Umm Qasr, marking a significant security incident affecting maritime operations in the region.

events.title: JAXPORT expands vehicle berth capacity
events.location: Blount Island Marine Terminal, Jacksonville, FL
events.description: Jacksonville Port Authority has reached the halfway point in constructing a new vehicle berth at Blount Island Marine Terminal. The project is part of a $60 million initiative to expand automotive handling capacity, with completion expected in early 2027.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz remains closed, disrupting shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, causing significant disruptions to global shipping and supply chains. Industry leaders highlighted the ongoing challenges during the Posidonia 2026 maritime exhibition in Athens.

events.title: CMA CGM implements East Africa PSS from China
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) for cargo moving from China to East Africa, effective from June 7 to June 15, 2026, depending on origin and destination.

events.title: Baltic Container Terminal upgrades with new quay cranes
events.description: Baltic Container Terminal in Gdynia has received two new quay cranes manufactured by ZMPC as part of a joint investment program with the Port of Gdynia Authority to modernize the port and enhance its role as a logistics hub in the Baltic Sea region.

events.title: Belgian transport firm Ziegler files for bankruptcy
events.description: Belgian transport company Ziegler has filed for bankruptcy, affecting four companies and around 400 employees. The firm's French subsidiary already went bankrupt in March.

events.title: MSC Boxship Damaged by Explosion Off Iraq
events.description: A Mediterranean Shipping Co (MSC) containership was damaged by an explosion approximately 40 nautical miles southeast of Iraq's Umm Qasr port, highlighting security risks in the Gulf region.

events.title: Baltimore Bridge Collapse Trial Delayed
events.description: A federal judge has postponed the civil trial related to the collapse of Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge after last-minute settlements resolved most remaining claims. The trial was expected to begin this week and had significant implications for supply chain operations in the region.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Affects Supply Chain
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed, disrupting global freight flows. Iranian officials indicated that an Israeli ceasefire is required to continue peace talks.

events.title: Royal Fleet Auxiliary Officers Plan 24-Hour Strike
events.description: The UK’s Royal Fleet Auxiliary is facing labor issues as officers plan a 24-hour strike, potentially disrupting naval support operations and related logistics.

events.title: COSCO-PTP JV to redevelop Tarragona Port
events.description: A joint venture between China COSCO and Spanish company PTP has been approved to redevelop and operate Tarragona Port in Spain. The concession marks a strategic move to modernize and expand port infrastructure.

events.title: MSC Boxship Holed in Attacks Off Iraq
events.description: An MSC containership was holed above the waterline following two attacks off the coast of Iraq, as reported by a pro-Iranian media outlet. The incident involves the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and raises concerns over regional security and maritime safety.

events.title: Cargo vessel struck near Iraq, raises Gulf shipping concerns
events.description: A cargo vessel was struck by an unidentified projectile approximately 40 nautical miles southeast of Umm Qasr on 1 June, causing a large explosion. The incident has raised concerns about maritime security in Gulf shipping routes.

events.title: MSC Containership Damaged in Apparent Attack Off Iraq
events.description: A Panama-flagged MSC containership was damaged in what appears to be an attack off Iraq, marking the third reported incident in the region since late February. The attack raises concerns about ongoing security risks affecting maritime freight flows.

events.title: US campaign to end Jones Act waiver
events.description: The American Maritime Partnership is urging the US administration to end the 150-day Jones Act waiver, claiming it is harming domestic maritime jobs, investment, and shipbuilding interests. The Jones Act requires cargo between US ports to be carried on US-built, -owned, and -crewed vessels.

events.title: France boards sanctioned shadow fleet tanker
events.description: French troops boarded the sanctioned crude oil tanker Tagor (114,800 dwt) on May 31, suspecting it of sailing under a false flag. The vessel is linked to the shadow fleet and is being investigated for potential sanctions violations.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd implements GRI for India/Pakistan to North America
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd is introducing a General Rate Increase (GRI) on container shipments from the Indian Subcontinent and Pakistan to the United States and Canada, effective July 1, 2026. The GRI applies to all container types and marks a carrier-driven pricing adjustment in the region.

events.title: ONE updates Europe Environment Surcharge to include UK
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) has expanded its Europe Environment Surcharge (EES) to include cargo moving in and out of the United Kingdom, effective July 1, 2026, following the extension of the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS).

events.title: Barcelona Port Road Junction Tender Launched
events.description: The Port of Barcelona has launched a tender for a EUR€98 million road junction project, aimed at improving traffic flow and supporting increased port activity.

events.title: RwandAir rethinks cargo routing due to Middle East disruption
events.description: Geopolitical instability in the Middle East is prompting RwandAir to reconsider cargo routing strategies, with Kigali emerging as an alternative hub to avoid exposed trade corridors.

events.title: US tariffs to sustain high ocean freight rates
events.description: Wan Hai Lines' GM predicts that US tariffs will continue to support elevated ocean freight rates into October. The 10% tariff on US imports has caused Asian shippers to front-load shipments ahead of its expiration.

events.title: Carriers hiking rates, cutting allocations in Asia-Europe trades
events.description: Ocean carriers are increasing spot market rates and reducing allocations for long-term contract shippers amid strong demand, leading to concerns over price-gouging and disrupted freight planning.

events.title: Rhine low water and port congestion disrupt barge traffic
events.description: Low water levels on the Rhine are restricting barge capacity, but port congestion and poor treatment of barges at terminals are exacerbating the issue. Water levels have fallen below 150cm, prompting low-water surcharges and operational challenges.

events.title: France intercepts Russian-linked shadow fleet tanker
events.description: France's navy intercepted a sanctioned tanker linked to Russian oil in the Atlantic Ocean, redirecting it to the French mainland. Russia called the action illegal and akin to 'international piracy.'

events.title: US and Iran Clash Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: US and Iranian forces clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, raising concerns over potential disruptions to maritime freight flows in the region.

events.title: France intercepts Russian shadow fleet tanker
events.description: French naval forces intercepted a Russian shadow fleet tanker in the Atlantic Ocean as part of an aggressive enforcement campaign to expand sanctions against Russia.

events.title: Shipping Hesitant to Return to Hormuz Despite Ceasefire
events.description: Shipping executives warn that despite a ceasefire in the region, confidence in the safety of the Strait of Hormuz remains low, and additional measures are needed to restore normal shipping flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz grain imports halted
events.description: GCC countries have not imported grain via the Strait of Hormuz since 28 February, disrupting major grain flows. Iran is importing more grain via Chabahar, but volumes are insufficient to offset the loss.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz security risks persist
events.description: Anglo-Eastern is reinforcing fleet security due to ongoing risks in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint facing prolonged compound security threats.

events.title: US Blockade Disrupts Venezuelan Crude Exports
events.description: A US-led blockade of sanctioned tankers has disrupted Venezuelan crude oil exports, causing a surge in trade volumes as vessels navigate geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transits limited amid US-Iran ceasefire extension
events.description: Transits through the Strait of Hormuz remain limited despite a 60-day ceasefire extension between the US and Iran. Iran has agreed to allow traffic to resume, but current restrictions continue to impact global freight flows.

events.title: Ukraine Damages Tanker and Oil Terminal in Crimea
events.description: Ukrainian drone forces attacked a product tanker and oil terminal in Russian-occupied Crimea, causing damage and disrupting energy infrastructure.

events.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged fire over the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in a key global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Political deadlock hinders Hormuz shipping
events.description: A recent poll indicates that political deadlock remains the primary barrier to normal shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, overshadowing security and transit concerns.

events.title: Port of Oakland sees modest TEU decline in April 2026
events.description: The Port of Oakland handled 184,492 TEUs in April 2026, a modest decrease from March's 198,667 TEUs, with vessel calls dropping from 86 to 80. Year-on-year volume was down just 0.5% compared to April 2025, indicating slight seasonal or operational shifts.

events.title: French Navy intercepts sanctioned Russian tanker Tagor
events.description: The French Navy intercepted the sanctioned Russian tanker Tagor in the Atlantic Ocean, more than 400 nautical miles west of Brittany. The vessel is suspected of being part of Russia’s shadow fleet, and its seizure could disrupt oil transportation and trade flows.

events.title: Global container repositioning reaches 30% of shipping work
events.description: Trade imbalances have increased the share of empty container shipments to 30% of global container shipping work, up from 25% before the pandemic, according to Sea-Intelligence analysis. This reflects ongoing supply chain inefficiencies and repositioning challenges in the container shipping industry.

events.title: Suspected mine spotted near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A floating object suspected to be a naval mine has been sighted in Omani territorial waters near the Strait of Hormuz. A navigation warning was issued, urging mariners to exercise extreme caution.

events.title: Port of Itajai removes 133-year-old wreck for dredging
events.description: The Port of Itajaí in Brazil is removing a 133-year-old shipwreck to prepare for dredging operations, which could impact local shipping and port activities during the process.

events.title: Conflict Continues in Gulf, No Ceasefire in Sight
events.description: Ongoing conflict in the Gulf region shows no signs of de-escalation, with continued tensions affecting the critical Strait of Hormuz, a major global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Drewry WCI rises for fourth straight week
events.description: The Drewry World Container Index (WCI) increased for the fourth consecutive week, reaching $2,800 per 40ft container. Rising rates are driven by early peak season demand, with Asia-Europe and Transpacific trades seeing carriers implement higher pricing and tighter capacity management.

events.title: Bulkers converted to container ships due to tonnage shortage
events.description: Ship owners are converting two open-hatch bulk carriers into container ships to address the shortage of available container ships. High charter rates and limited newbuild deliveries until 2029 are driving this unconventional industry move.

events.title: Iran resumes gas production at South Pars platforms
events.description: Iran has resumed gas production at three offshore platforms in the South Pars gas field, which had been halted due to Israeli attacks disrupting processing capacity.

events.title: Suspected Mine Detected Near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Oman’s Maritime Security Centre issued a navigation warning after a floating object suspected to be a naval mine was sighted near the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global shipping.

events.title: China conducts patrols near Scarborough Shoal
events.description: China's military and coast guard conducted patrols near Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea, following a warning from the Philippines about ongoing threats in the disputed waters.

events.title: U.S. Disables Vessel Near Iran in Ongoing Blockade
events.description: U.S. forces disabled a commercial vessel in the Gulf of Oman after it allegedly ignored warnings and approached an Iranian port, indicating the continuation of the U.S. blockade despite ongoing peace talks.

events.title: Mexican truckers lose US visas over cabotage violations
events.description: Thousands of Mexican truckers have lost their US visas due to cabotage violations, disrupting cross-border freight movements. The issue arises from enforcement actions related to unauthorized hauling within the US.

events.title: MSC adds Napier to Eagle service rotation
events.description: MSC has added Napier, New Zealand, to its Eagle service rotation, which connects the US East Coast with Australia and New Zealand, effective in June. The update reflects a shift in shipping routes to enhance connectivity in the region.

events.title: Samskip adds Halmstad to Europe–Iceland service
events.description: Samskip will add Halmstad to its Blue service connecting Europe and Iceland, starting in September. The service uses two 900 TEU vessels and expands network coverage in Scandinavia.

events.title: US Disables Cargo Ship in Gulf of Oman
events.description: U.S. forces disabled a small cargo ship on May 29 after it ignored warnings, according to CENTCOM. The incident occurred in the Gulf of Oman, a key maritime chokepoint.

events.title: Ukrainian drones strike Russian port and oil depot
events.description: Ukrainian drone strikes damaged a port and oil refinery in Taganrog and an oil depot in Armavir, disrupting Russian oil infrastructure and potentially affecting regional freight and logistics flows.

events.title: Rio de Janeiro port upgrades for larger vessels
events.location: Port of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
events.description: The Port of Rio de Janeiro has completed a USD 32 million dredging and engineering project, allowing it to accommodate vessels up to 366 metres in length and increasing its operational capacity.

events.title: Hormuz Strait closure impacts fleet operations
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz enters its fourth month, affecting 16 Anglo-Eastern vessels and over 350 seafarers. The company is enhancing fleet security via its Global Security Desk to monitor threats and support crews.

events.title: Porsche Wins Lawsuit Over Felicity Ace Fire
events.description: A German court ruled in favor of Porsche in a lawsuit regarding the fire on the car carrier Felicity Ace, which caused significant cargo loss. The cause of the fire remains unproven, impacting maritime freight and insurance claims.

events.title: U.S. Warns Ships in Hormuz of Hostile Treatment
events.description: The U.S. has warned commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz that ignoring military orders may result in being treated as hostile targets, raising concerns about operational safety and potential disruptions to shipping.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz standoff, now in its fourth month, involves mutual blockades with Iran charging ships up to $2 million for safe passage, while the U.S. enforces its own restrictions, causing significant disruption to global shipping.

events.title: EU plans new steel tariffs, negotiations with China
events.description: The European Union plans to implement new steel tariffs starting July 1, prompting negotiations with China under the World Trade Organization. China's Ministry of Commerce has criticized the move as trade protectionism.

events.title: Increased dark transits in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Vortexa has observed a significant rise in dark transits through the Strait of Hormuz, with 57% of all transits since March being dark. The trend reflects heightened uncertainty and potential risks to global oil and freight flows.

events.title: Russian attacks on merchant ships near Odesa injure crew
events.description: A Turkish-owned cargo ship was set on fire, and two crew members were injured near Odesa as Russia continues attacks on merchant vessels in the region.

events.title: US ports face $6.7bn investment gap
events.description: The National Association of Waterfront Employers (NAWE) reports a $6.7 billion investment gap in US ports, threatening competitiveness and supply chain resilience. The survey highlights urgent modernization and infrastructure needs across major ports.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Disruptions Drain Oil Inventories
events.description: The heads of four major global economic and energy institutions issued a joint warning about ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, which are rapidly depleting oil inventories at a record pace.

events.title: COSCO Tanker Arrives in Connecticut under Jones Act Waiver
events.description: A Chinese-owned COSCO tanker delivered asphalt to Connecticut under a Trump-era Jones Act waiver, reigniting debate over the policy's impact on maritime trade and supply chain operations.

events.title: Restricted Area for Drone Performance in Hong Kong
events.location: New Hung Hom Harbourfront, Hong Kong
events.description: A restricted area will be in place off the New Hung Hom Harbourfront in Hong Kong from 1800 to 2200 hours on May 30, 2026, for a drone performance. This may cause minor disruptions to vessel movements in the area during the event.

events.title: Cable Layer Collides with Boxship off Pakistan
events.description: A cable layer was holed in a collision with a containership off the coast of Pakistan on May 28. Officials are investigating the incident, which occurred as both vessels were maneuvering outside the port area.

events.title: Evergreen and T.S. Lines slot swap on Asia-Japan services
events.description: Evergreen Marine Corporation and T.S. Lines have entered into a slot swap agreement on their JPI and JHTN services connecting China, Taiwan, and Japan. The arrangement allows both carriers to expand network coverage without deploying additional vessels.

events.title: Vessels attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Several vessels transiting through the Strait of Hormuz have been attacked in recent days, highlighting significant risks to global shipping and supply chain flows.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping status in question
events.description: President Trump claimed a breakthrough regarding the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting Iran must allow unrestricted commercial shipping. However, questions remain about the actual status and enforceability of shipping access through this critical global trade chokepoint.

events.title: Big Oil Tankers Escape Iran War Blockade
events.description: Roughly one-quarter of non-Iranian large oil tankers trapped in the Persian Gulf due to the Iran war have managed to escape in a slow, stealthy manner.

events.title: UK bans Russian LNG maritime transport
events.description: The UK introduced new sanctions prohibiting the maritime transportation of Russian liquefied natural gas (LNG), strengthening its existing restrictions on Russian energy exports.

events.title: Ocean Alliance revises CBX service route
events.description: The Ocean Alliance has revised the Chesapeake Bay Express (CBX) service, adding Port Kelang back to the rotation and including New York. The service is operated by CMA CGM on behalf of the alliance.

events.title: Medkon launches Far East–Mediterranean service
events.description: Medkon Lines is set to launch a new Far East–Mediterranean connection with its BTN service, starting with a single sailing from Shanghai and Ningbo to Mediterranean ports.

events.title: EU customs rule change risks parcel disruption
events.description: DHL, FedEx, and UPS warn that the EU's new customs rules for low-value parcels, including the removal of the €150 de minimis exemption and a €3 flat-rate tax, could cause border disruptions starting 1 July.

events.title: Freight rate spike expected due to global supply chain pressures
events.description: Freight rates are expected to rise significantly as global supply chain disruptions intensify, with carriers likely to benefit from increased demand and constrained liner networks.

events.title: Shipping carriers impose 'pay to play' pricing on contracts
events.description: Shipping carriers are enforcing peak season surcharges on long-term contracts due to high demand and tight capacity, forcing shippers to pay higher prices to secure freight space. Forwarders warn that spot rates on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes may rise sharply in the coming weeks.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz ceasefire extension optimism
events.description: Growing optimism over a potential US-Iran deal to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is weighing on energy markets, with implications for global oil supply stability.

events.title: Houston Ship Channel vessel activity rises to seven-year high
events.description: Vessel activity along the Houston Ship Channel has increased by 5% compared to the same period in 2025, with over 2,000 arrivals recorded in Q1 2026, reflecting growing cargo volumes through the channel.

events.title: US warns Oman on Hormuz Strait toll proposal
events.description: The U.S. warned Oman against participating in a proposed toll system for the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint. The warning highlights potential policy shifts that could disrupt maritime trade flows through the region.

events.title: Tight bunker fuel availability in Houston
events.description: Bunker fuel availability in the US Gulf port of Houston has become tight, with suppliers requiring reconfirmation of offers due to ongoing market volatility. Demand remains strong, particularly affecting HSFO and LSMGO lead times.

events.title: ARA bunker availability tightens due to Hormuz disruption
events.description: Ongoing disruption near the Strait of Hormuz has caused loading delays and fuel blending issues, tightening bunker availability in the ARA region. Lead times for VLSFO and HSFO have doubled, with buyers advised to book 10 days in advance for competitive offers.

events.title: Increased Hormuz Strait ship transits on May 26
events.description: Ship transits through the Strait of Hormuz increased to 13 on May 26, with seven ships inbound and six outbound, according to CAS data.

events.title: CMA CGM increases Asia-Mediterranean FAK rates
events.description: CMA CGM announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for cargo from Asian main ports to Mediterranean and North African destinations, effective from 15 to 31 June 2026.

events.title: Congestion at JNPA's largest container terminal
events.description: PSA International's BMCT terminal at Nhava Sheva Port is experiencing cargo handling delays and gate congestion due to a surge in transhipment volumes, despite improvements at other JNPA terminals.

events.title: Felicity Ace Fire Lawsuit Dismissed
events.description: A court dismissed a lawsuit against Porsche related to the fire aboard the car carrier Felicity Ace. The incident had previously caused a major disruption in the transport of vehicles.

events.title: CMA CGM implements new PSS for Africa, Mediterranean, Maldives routes
events.description: CMA CGM has introduced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) for routes from China and Asia to West Africa, the Mediterranean, and the Maldives, effective 1 June 2026, impacting dry and reefer cargo under short-term contracts.

events.title: Yiulian Dockyard Reopens with Laden Vessel Repair Capability
events.description: Hong Kong's Yiulian Dockyard has reopened after modernization, offering a unique capability to repair laden vessels. Operated by China Merchants Shipbuilding Industry, it is now positioned as a highly flexible repair facility in Asia.

events.title: Hormuz shipping crisis nears 100 days
events.description: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz shows no sign of resolution, with tensions between the US and Iran escalating. The ongoing situation continues to pose a significant risk to global shipping.
